title: Creamy Peppery Filipino Mixed Grain Pilaf
description: A comforting Filipino-inspired pilaf dish featuring a mix of grains, pickled vegetables, and a rich creamy sauce, all infused with the deep flavor of fresh black pepper. This recipe is a sugar-free-friendly twist on a classic Filipino recipe.

ingredients.name: mixed grain blend (jasmine and Calrose rice, black rice, and red rice)
black peppercorns
ingredients.notes: grind using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle before using

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and saut4e until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the pickled jalape8o pepper and saut4e for another minute, until slightly caramelized.
Add the mixed grain blend and stir to coat with oil and mix with ginger and pickled jalape8o.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover the saucepan.
Simmer the grain mixture for 18 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the grains are tender.
While the grains cook, grind the black peppercorns using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the coconut milk and a pinch of salt.
After the grains have cooked, fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the coconut milk mixture and ground black pepper.
Season the pilaf with salt to taste, then serve hot and enjoy.
